findings.reachability: Any authenticated WordPress user (subscriber+) can trigger wp-admin/admin-ajax.php?action=flexible_shipping_rate_notice or ?action=flexible_shipping_close_rate_notice
findings.justification: add_action registers two AJAX callbacks (wp_ajax_flexible_shipping_rate_notice, wp_ajax_flexible_shipping_close_rate_notice) pointing to methods that do not exist on the class, causing a PHP fatal error on every invocation; this can be used to trigger repeated error-log writes or exhaust error-log space but does not expose data or bypass authorization.

detection_pattern.section_name: Dead AJAX handler: registered but unimplemented method
detection_pattern.description: Finds wp_ajax_ registrations that bind to $this methods; pair with absence of matching function definition to detect handlers that will fatal-error when invoked. A DoS primitive for logged-in users and causes noisy error logs.
